data.title: Financial Advisors and Large Brokerage Firms Push for Next Wave of Bitcoin ETF Adoption
data.description:
Source: cryptoslate

Bloomberg ETF analyst James Seyffart believes that the next phase of cryptocurrency exchange-traded funds (ETFs) will be driven by financial advisors, large securities firms, and brokers that manage assets for high-net-worth individuals.

Seyffart outlined in the "Coin Stories" podcast how these financial institutions, which manage trillions of dollars in assets, will play a central role in expanding the Bitcoin ETF market.

He noted that Bitcoin (BTC) ETFs have performed exceptionally strongly in their first year on the market, exceeding many analysts' expectations. Although Bloomberg remains optimistic about these ETFs, he admitted that the actual performance exceeded their expectations.  

Seyffart said:

“There have been some outflows in recent weeks, but since launch, they’ve only had $40 billion at their highest and $110 billion in assets. IBIT is one of the most traded ETFs, and it’s hit $50 billion in a few hundred days, and the previous record was over a thousand days. So, no matter how you look at it, they’ve broken every record you can see.”

Given this momentum, he recommends a gradual adoption of Bitcoin ETFs as a portfolio allocation tool for high-net-worth clients to drive continued success.

Big Players Enter

While large firms such as BlackRock recommend allocating 1% to 2% of BTC in a portfolio, Seyffart stressed that “large institutions and large banks” are not allowing investors to buy cryptocurrency ETFs.

He added that large brokerage firms, financial advisors, and brokerage platforms control a large amount of capital for ultra-wealthy investors, including billionaires.  

These entities influence asset allocation decisions across a broad range of financial portfolios. Seyffart said that if these institutions began to include Bitcoin ETFs as a subsidiary portion of their portfolios (e.g. 5%), it could lead to continued growth in adoption.

In addition to institutional adoption, Seyffart also pointed out that corporations, states, and even countries are adding Bitcoin to their balance sheets. This could contribute to the legitimacy and stability of Bitcoin as an asset class in traditional finance.  

However, he stressed that increased acceptance by financial intermediaries could be the main driver of ETF growth.

data.title: Elon Musk’s D.O.G.E Eyes 20% Internal Revenue Service Staff Reduction: Are More Terminations on the Horizon?
data.description:
Elon Musk’s D.O.G.E Plans to Axe 20% of IRS Staff

The Department of Government Efficiency (D.O.G.E) is reportedly planning a 20% reduction in the Internal Revenue Service’s (IRS) workforce, affecting approximately 6,800 employees, with the changes expected by 15 May 2025.

This follows the earlier removal of 6,700 probationary workers and 4,700 IRS agents who received retirement severance packages.

BREAKING: Elon Musk's DOGE is proposing a 20% cut to IRS staff as per CNN. pic.twitter.com/OaEnebhbTL

— DogeDesigner (@cb_doge) March 13, 2025

However, a recent ruling by US District Judge William Alsup, which mandates the reinstatement of probationary employees terminated under D.O.G.E’s cost-cutting initiatives, could potentially delay or complicate these layoffs, depending on whether the ruling stands.

President Donald Trump has vowed to pursue comprehensive tax reform, which may include eliminating the federal income tax and funding the government solely through tariffs on foreign goods.

D.O.G.E’s Pursuit of Efficient Cost-Cutting

(D.O.G.E), led by Elon Musk, is exploring strategies to reduce the $36 trillion US national debt by significantly downsizing the federal bureaucracy and implementing cost-cutting reforms.

One notable proposal includes placing all public spending on the blockchain to enhance transparency and reduce deficits.

On 21 February, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) announced plans to cut its regional office directors in alignment with cost-saving directives from the Trump administration.

Despite these cuts, the regional offices across major US cities will remain operational, and the SEC has submitted a 2025 budget proposal to Congress requesting $2.6 billion.

BREAKING: The SEC plans to terminate all 10 regional directors ahead of bombshell @DOGE_SEC findings which are due to come in the next several days.

If they’re already cutting staff — there’s likely going to be some big waste reported by DOGE. pic.twitter.com/ggksfoK6NU

— Reese Politics (@ReesePolitics) February 25, 2025

Both President Trump and Musk have discussed redirecting 20% of D.O.G.E's savings to Americans through stimulus checks or tax credits.

Research from accounting automation firm Dancing Numbers suggests that Trump's plan to eliminate the federal income tax could save the average American $134,809 in lifetime taxes, potentially rising to $325,561 if state-level wage taxes are also repealed.

Not Everyone is Pro-Trump/Musk/D.O.G.E

Despite these efforts, not everyone is persuaded by D.O.G.E’s cost-cutting approach, particularly US Senator Elizabeth Warren.

I'm voting NO on the Republican shutdown bill.

NO on handing Donald Trump and Elon Musk a blank check to spend your taxpayer money however they want.

NO on cutting funding that helps families put food on the table and crack down on wealthy tax cheats.

We need to push back.

— Elizabeth Warren (@SenWarren) March 13, 2025

A vocal critic of Musk, Trump, and D.O.G.E, Warren expressed her concerns in January 2025.

In a letter to DOGE, she proposed increasing taxes and federal spending as a means to improve government efficiency, offering a stark contrast to the organisation's cost-reduction strategy.

data.title: Nearly 40% of Wealthy US Investors Plan to Buy Crypto, Grayscale Survey Reveals
data.description:
Wealthy Investors in the US Are Turning to Crypto Amid Economic Uncertainty

A growing number of high-net-worth investors in the United States are showing interest in cryptocurrency, according to a new survey by asset management firm Grayscale.

The report found that 26% of investors with over $1 million in investable assets already hold crypto, while an even larger share—38%—are planning to invest in the future.

This suggests a shift in investment behaviour, particularly among younger investors.

Younger Investors View Crypto as a Mainstream Asset

The survey reveals a generational divide in the perception of cryptocurrency.

Over half (53%) of high-net-worth individuals under 50 expect to include digital assets in their investment strategies, compared to just 22% of those over 50.

For younger investors, crypto is increasingly seen as a standard part of a diversified portfolio rather than a hedge against economic instability.

Peter Mintzberg, CEO of Grayscale, commented on the findings, saying,

“It’s exciting to witness the momentum shift in crypto as more investors begin to recognize the value of digital assets. Remarkably, 38% of high-net-worth investors believe their investment portfolio will include crypto in the future.”

Older Investors Turn to Bitcoin as an Inflation Hedge

While younger investors are drawn to crypto as a normalised asset class, 78% of wealthy investors over 50 who already own digital assets cite economic concerns as their main motivation.

Bitcoin, in particular, is viewed as a store of value amid inflation, geopolitical tensions, and the weakening US dollar.

A separate finding shows that 36% of high-net-worth investors, regardless of age, are paying closer attention to Bitcoin due to these macroeconomic factors.

This reinforces Bitcoin’s long-standing narrative as a hedge against traditional financial instability.

Wealthy Investors Struggle to See Crypto’s Use Beyond Investment

Despite growing interest, nearly half of surveyed investors remain sceptical about cryptocurrency’s utility beyond financial speculation.

While crypto advocates envision a future where blockchain technology powers decentralised finance, smart contracts, and other innovations, many high-net-worth investors view it simply as a means to increase fiat wealth.

This division is becoming more apparent, with some industry observers concerned that a focus on financial gains could stifle technological progress.

The recent backlash to former President Trump’s Crypto Summit further illustrates the differing opinions within the space.

Crypto’s Role in Wealth Management Is Expanding

Grayscale’s survey, which polled 5,368 adults who participated in the last US presidential election, reflects both optimism and challenges for the industry.

On one hand, increased interest from wealthy investors signals potential large-scale inflows into the market, particularly during periods of economic uncertainty.

On the other hand, the perception of crypto as a mere investment vehicle rather than a technological revolution suggests that the industry still faces hurdles in convincing mainstream investors of its broader applications.

As the sector evolves, projects aiming to bridge this gap may determine the future direction of cryptocurrency adoption.

data.title: Solo Bitcoin Miner Defy All Odds And Hits The Jackpot With Pocket-Sized Rig, Earning $263K Block Reward
data.description:
In an industry that leverage on large-scale mining pools and powerful hashing power, a solo Bitcoin miner is determined to show that he could achieve the same feat with just a cheap, pocket-sized crypto mining rig.

How long does it take to mine a #BTC block?

On March 10, a solo miner struck gold, solving a Bitcoin block with a $299 device and just 0.48 TH/s of hash power, earning 3.15 BTC (~$260K).

The odds of this happening? 1 in 1.6 billion. Or roughly once every 31,240 years if mining… pic.twitter.com/dkE3RrgDaj

— Panzuki.eth⚡️ (@PandaAsiaStreet) March 14, 2025

This miner became the 297th solo participant to mine a Bitcoin block through the solo.ckpool mining pool, securing a massive $263,000 reward, according to solo.ckpool developer, Con Kolivas.

Hiting the jackpot despite a 1 in a million feat

Kolivas revealed that the miner was using a 480-gigahash per second (GH/s) Bitaxe machine, a relatively modest setup compared to industrial-scale mining operations, which often deploy machines exceeding 230,000 GH/s.

Kolivas also revealed that this miner had achieved a 1-in-a-million feat as he had mined a block as a solo miner. This device solved the #887,212 block and won $263K worth of BTC.

The block was mined on March 10 at 7.22pm UTC, and the miner was rewarded with 3.15 BTC, which includes a 3.125 BTC mining reward plus an additional 0.025 BTC transaction fee.

This marks an extraordinary feat, as it is speculated that the miner was using the least powerful model in the brand, called the "Generic Solo Miner Lucky Miner."

A solo #Bitcoin miner with just 3.3 TH/s has mined a block!#BTC miners usually rely on massive, noisy, and power-hungry machines—yet even with all that, they often don’t expect to mine a block themselves. Instead, they join mining pools, where computing power is combined, and… pic.twitter.com/VInNdImFKD

— CAN (Crypto ANalysis) (@COINEO963) March 12, 2025

A 1,200 gigahash Bitaxe Gamma 601 machine, which is three times more powerful than the one used by the miner, would have cost three times of what the miner was using.

David in front of Golliath

In today's crypto-mining industry, corporations often choose mining devices with more than 230,000 GH/s of power. This has made it almost impossible for lone miners, and even those with tiny mining rigs to solve a block.

This is especially true when we take into account the size of the mining device the lone miner was using, the chance is lower than one in a million per day. This equals to 3,500 years of mining for an average miner using this device.

Furthermore, many miners also often choose to mine from large Bitcoin mining pools. As such, most of the hashrate from public BTC Bitfarms, Cipher, and Hut8 is directed to the Foundry USA.

Very interesting website ( https://t.co/G5P4q9aDFV ) to compare mining profitability on all the different #Bitcoin pools. pic.twitter.com/srJvgF0EHr

— WhalePanda (@WhalePanda) December 26, 2017

The current largest mining corporation is held be the largest mining corporation, MARA Holdings. MARA also has the highest market cap and hashrate, gaining the top position in mining.

Bitcoin mining difficulties are hitting new highs. Can solo miners even survive?

The success story of this miner comes at a time when mining has become more competitive than ever. Hashrate Asset Group posted a report on its social media account, where it shows how the Bitcoin hashrate hit 807. 517EH/s in the past week.

This monthly report provides a detailed analysis of February's performance. Due to external factors, HAG's yield was affected to some extent.

The team acknowledges this issue and plans to enhance mining operations and marketing strategies.

Full Report:https://t.co/zGFJmkZ3xR

— HAG - Hashrate Asset Group (@HashrateAsset) March 13, 2025

This marks a 1.22% increase, suggesting that there could be new miners who are entering the network. But this would also imply that the chances of solo miners solving a block are now even lower than before.

To make matters worse, mining difficulty has also recently increased. The BTC mining difficulty now stands at 112.150T, meaning it is now harder to solve a block.

The #Bitcoin mining difficulty continues to rise, and we know what that means…

The #Bitcoin top isn't in yet! 🚀 pic.twitter.com/o91dAawloz

— Coinvo (@ByCoinvo) March 13, 2025

Although the Bitcoin block reward saw a 0.09% increase, the average price of Bitcoin decreased from $86.713.20 to $85,069.54. This decline is more important to individual miners as they bank on BTC price growth to offset the hardware costs.

In a world that is dominated by large firms, it seems that the chances of survival for individual miners is very very low. While cost-efficient devices like Bitaxe can make solo mining profitable in rare cases, it is not a reliable source of steady income.

Mining pools offer a more consistent approach, providing frequent but smaller payouts and reducing financial risk. As the upcoming Bitcoin halving cuts block rewards, miners must focus on efficiency and optimization to sustain profitability in an increasingly competitive landscape.

data.title: Huobi HTX lending and mining and other multi-dimensional investment tools help users win steadily
data.description:
In early March 2025, Bitcoin experienced the largest single-day market value growth in history, and only one day later, it ushered in the second largest single-day decline in history. Many traders suffered serious losses in this environment. With frequent price fluctuations and intensified market fluctuations, how to find a stable profit path in uncertainty has become the focus of many users.

Therefore, with the continuous development of the cryptocurrency market, pledge lending, as one of the important financial tools, has received increasing attention-it enables users to hold assets while maintaining liquidity.

Large amount of loans, ultra-low interest rates, borrow and repay at any time, Huobi HTX's "Borrowing is Mining Prize Pool Sharing Event" helps users to survive market fluctuations

In line with this trend, Huobi HTX launched its star product, the "Borrowing is Mining" activity, which provides users with an ideal choice to survive market fluctuations.

Currently, Huobi HTX's "Borrowing is Mining" activity has been successfully held for four sessions. The fifth session will be opened for a limited time from 10:00 on March 14 to 23:59 on March 31 (UTC+8) on March 25, with a high prize pool of 5,000,000,000 $HTX, providing users with a low-risk, high-return investment option, helping users to win steadily in market fluctuations.

During the event, users who participate in the current USDT lending product can share the prize pool according to the proportion of interest expenditure. The greater the proportion of accumulated interest expenditure, the more rewards will be shared. The rewards will be distributed to the spot account within 7 working days after the end of the event.

It is worth mentioning that Huobi HTX pledged lending supports users to enjoy ultra-low interest rates for large-scale lending, and can borrow and repay at any time, with flexible operation, which can meet the diverse lending needs of users.

In the future, Huobi HTX pledged lending products will continue to iterate and upgrade, and plans to introduce a multi-currency pledge mechanism in the second quarter of this year to optimize user capital utilization and lending experience. At the same time, it will continue to optimize the interest rate mechanism and reward model, launch a loan discount coupon system, provide users with more diversified discounts, and further reduce borrowing costs.  

The market is changing, and Huobi HTX's various investment tools make wealth more stable

Under the combined effect of multiple negative factors such as macroeconomic uncertainty and frustrated confidence in the crypto market, investors' risk aversion has increased. Huobi HTX responds quickly to market changes, launches and optimizes a variety of products to help users find a balance in turbulence.

As one of Huobi HTX's core coin-earning products, USDD has become a risk-averse tool in volatile markets with its 1:1 anchoring to the US dollar. At present, the USDT subscription function of USDD current coin-earning has been officially launched, supporting USDT 1:1 lossless subscription, with an annualized return of up to 12%.

In addition, for users who want to actively capture market fluctuations, Huobi HTX's contract grid tool provides another option. As an arbitrage artifact in the current volatile market, the contract grid helps users earn dual benefits of trends and fluctuations.

Huobi HTX said that in the face of the normalized trend of intensified market volatility, it will continue to pay attention to market dynamics, continuously innovate products and services, and provide users with a safe, convenient and professional digital asset investment experience.

data.title: Hamster Network’s Future in Jeopardy as Transaction Volume Tanks, Plummets 99.9% in Two Weeks —Is Demise Inevitable?
data.description:
Transaction Volume of Hamster Network Drops Drastically

Hamster Kombat’s Layer-2 blockchain, once surpassing Solana in transactions per second (TPS), is now facing a steep decline.

Just two weeks after launch, the Hamster Network’s transaction volume has plummeted by 99.9%, sparking concerns over its sustainability.

Initially designed as a gaming-focused blockchain for dApps within the “Hamsterverse,” the network peaked at 34,028 TPS in early March—3.5 times Solana’s throughput.

🚀 34,028 TPS achieved by Hamster Network!

That’s 3.5x faster than Solana! Big things are coming! Hamster Network is about to launch an accelerator program for builders to create truly decentralized applications—free from high gas fees and scalability issues.

Why Hamster… pic.twitter.com/pj9ZjKSOr1

— Hamster Kombat (@hamster_kombat) March 4, 2025

However, by 14 March, it had fallen to just 2.74 TPS, processing only 59,615 transactions in 24 hours, a stark contrast to its 150 million peak on launch day.

This dramatic drop raises pressing questions about its long-term viability.

Hamster Kombat Shows Significant Decline in User Engagement

Hamster Kombat's once-thriving user base is rapidly shrinking, mirroring the network’s broader decline.

The game, which boasted over 300 million monthly players, now struggles to retain engagement.

As of 26 February 2025, active users had dropped to 11.5 million, but within two weeks, that number fell further to 10.3 million—a loss of 1.2 million players.

hamster network transaction volume down 99.9 percent two weeks post launch, from 34021 tps to 0.84 tps, monthly users dropped from 300 million to 10 million, insiders say airdrops fueled initial hype, not genuine interest, keep an eye on developments

— Meme Coin Mania (@MemeCoinMania77) March 13, 2025

New account creation has also stalled, with only 12 sign-ups, highlighting a sharp decline in interest.

Once a major player in the clicker or tap-to-earn gaming space, Hamster Kombat now faces mounting concerns over its long-term viability.

Is Hamster Kombat Destined for Doom or Will it Regain its Former Glory?

Industry analysts attribute Hamster Kombat’s early success to the hype surrounding its crypto airdrops rather than genuine engagement with its blockchain.

Like many play-to-earn and tap-to-earn games, it has struggled to retain players once incentives tapered off.

Despite this, the Hamster Kombat team maintains that the L2 network is still in its early stages and will undergo further stress tests to assess its full potential.

However, the sharp declines in transaction volume and player activity underscore the volatility of blockchain gaming.

Whether the team can reignite momentum—or if developers and gamers will find lasting value in the platform—remains uncertain.

data.title: The next stage of BTC ETF: Financial advisors and large institutions lead the expansion trend
data.description:
‍Bloomberg's exchange-traded fund (ETF) analyst James Seyffart believes that the next phase of adoption of crypto asset ETFs will be driven by financial advisors, large brokerage firms and brokers who manage assets for high-net-worth individuals.

In an episode of the "Coin Stories" podcast, Seyffart outlined how these financial institutions, which manage trillions of dollars in assets, can play a central role in expanding the BTC ETF market.

He noted that the BTC ETF's first year in the market was exceptionally strong, exceeding the expectations of many analysts. While Bloomberg is optimistic about the BTC ETF, he admitted that the actual performance exceeded their predictions.

Seyffart said: "There have been some outflows in recent weeks, but since launch, the size of funds has only decreased by about $40 billion at its peak, and the assets of these ETFs are currently around $110 billion. IBIT has been one of the most actively traded ETFs, reaching a size of $50 billion in just a hundred days, and the previous record was more than a thousand days. So no matter how you look at it, they have broken all the records that can be seen."

Given this momentum, he believes that the use of BTC ETFs as a tool for high-net-worth clients to allocate their portfolios may drive their continued success.

Although large companies like BlackRock recommend allocating 1% to 2% of BTC in a portfolio, Seyffart stressed that "large brokerage firms and large banks" do not allow investors to buy Crypto asset ETFs.

He added that these brokerage firms, financial advisors, and brokerage platforms control a lot of money, including billionaires.

These institutions influence asset allocation decisions in a wide range of financial portfolios. Seyffart said that if these institutions begin to include BTC ETFs as part of their portfolios, such as 5%, this could drive continued growth in adoption.

In addition to institutional adoption, Seyffart also noticed a trend of companies, states, and even countries including BTC on their balance sheets. This helps to enhance the legitimacy and stability of BTC as an asset class in the traditional financial sector.

However, he emphasized that increased acceptance by financial intermediaries may be a key driver of ETF growth.

Today, asset management company Grayscale released a report claiming that high-net-worth investors have taken an interest in Crypto assets.

Grayscale CEO Peter Mintzberg said on social media: "It is great to see that the momentum of Crypto assets is shifting, and more and more investors are beginning to recognize the value of digital assets. It is worth noting that 38% of high net worth investors believe that their portfolios will include Crypto assets in the future."

How about a "like, share, and recommend"?

data.title: Breaking News: Trump reportedly buys stake in Binance
data.description:
Source: Wall Street Journal

Trump invests in Binance

The Wall Street Journal reported thatBinance reporter Zhao Changpeng (CZ) is actively seeking pardon from the Trump administration after completing his 4-month sentence in order to re-expand Binance's US market. (Changpeng is still the largest shareholder of Binance, but is restricted from permanently participating in the company's shareholders. The regulations also have an impact on directors with felony criminal convictions))

Binance attempts to enter the US market

According to people familiar with the matter, Binance began to actively contact the Trump family last year, hoping to conclude an alliance with the Trump family and re-enter the US market.

It is not clear in what form the Trump family will hold shares in Binance US. One possible way is for the Trump family to hold shares directly, and another is through World Liberty Financial, which was established in September last year.

After being convicted, Zhao Changpeng still seeks a comeback in Binance US

Recall that Binance agreed to pay a sky-high fine of US$4.3 billion in 2023 to resolve allegations of violations by US regulators. At that time, Zhao Changpeng was forced to resign as CEO but retained a majority stake.

For Binance, if Zhao Changpeng can obtain a pardon from the Trump administration, it may improve Binance US's business development in the international market.

After Binance's US business was significantly reduced due to regulatory pressure in 2023, people familiar with the matter revealed that Binance has set up a working group led by current CEO Richard Teng to evaluate different options. Pardons may help Binance repair its US business damaged by long-term legal disputes.

People familiar with the matter also said that Binance had studied Sun Yuchen's legal strategy internally. The cryptocurrency entrepreneur became its largest investor by investing $30 million in World Liberty Financial when facing charges from the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Last month, the SEC Asking the court to suspend the fraud lawsuit against Justin Sun has drawn attention to the role of the Trump administration.

Trump family and Binance transactions

People familiar with the matter pointed out that World Liberty Financial, co-founded by Trump's two sons and his friend Witkoff, may become a direct beneficiary if the transaction is conducted through the company.

A White House official refuted Tuli's claim and said that Witkoff participated in the Israeli-Pakistani ceasefire negotiations in the Middle East this week and went to Moscow on Thursday, where he might meet with Russian President Putin.

It is understood that the Trump administration is still in talks with Binance. In addition, the SEC also announced last month that it would suspend another lawsuit against Binance, which is believed to be related to the Trump administration's shift in cryptocurrency policy.

The Trump family's entry may help it turn around

Binance US once reached a valuation of US$4.5 billion in 2022, but due to regulatory issues, its market share plummeted from 27% in 2023 to 1%, resulting in a large number of senior executives leaving the company.

Binance believes that if Zhao Changpeng is pardoned, it will be able to remove regulatory barriers to its US business. The pardon will also open the door to the European Union, where companies whose shareholders have been convicted of crimes such as money laundering will be prohibited from applying for crypto regulatory licenses.

At present, World Liberty Financial, the Trump family and Binance have not responded to the transaction, but if the case continues to move forward, it will inevitably trigger greater controversy about the intersection of Trump's business and political interests.

data.title: Trump-Binance Speculation Rages On Despite CZ’s Pushback and Slamming Mainstream Media
data.description:
CZ Refutes WSJ Claims of a Binance-Trump Deal & Presidential Pardon

Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) is pushing back against a Wall Street Journal (WSJ) report alleging he is seeking a presidential pardon and that Binance.US has engaged in investment talks with the Trump family.

The article claims that Trump associates have discussed taking a stake in Binance.US and that CZ, as the company’s largest shareholder, is seeking clemency to re-enter the US market.

Exclusive: Trump family representatives have held talks to invest in Binance's U.S. arm, as the crypto exchange's founder seeks a pardon for his 2023 conviction https://t.co/dq26OoKYLB

— The Wall Street Journal (@WSJ) March 13, 2025

CZ, who pleaded guilty to money laundering charges in November 2023 and served four months in prison, firmly denies these claims.

In a 13 March post on X (formerly known as Twitter), he dismissed the WSJ report as false, asserting that he had never discussed any Binance.US deal with the Trump family and had not sought a pardon.

He accused the media of fabricating narratives to undermine both cryptocurrency and Trump’s administration, emphasizing that WSJ failed to present concrete evidence.

According to CZ, WSJ contacted at least 20 sources in an attempt to corroborate its claims but provided no definitive proof.

4. Sorry to disappoint. The WSJ article got the facts wrong.

More than 20 people have told me they were asked by the WSJ (and another media), "Can you confirm that CZ made some deal for a pardon?"

They probably asked hundreds of people to have 20 people reach out to me. In… https://t.co/ELyDPmKD3G

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) March 13, 2025

The report also suggests that Binance sought to re-establish its US presence following legal troubles and explored a potential deal through World Liberty Financial (WLF), a crypto venture linked to the Trump family.

It further alleges that Trump associate Steve Witkoff was involved in discussions, though an administration official denied this, stating Witkoff was in the process of divesting from business interests.

WSJ also reported that Binance formally requested a presidential pardon for CZ but noted it was “unclear” whether Trump’s potential investment in Binance.US would hinge on such a pardon.

If granted, a pardon could allow Binance to expand its operations in both the US and Europe.

Discussions between Binance and the Trump family reportedly began around Trump’s 5 November election victory, with sources claiming Binance executives were inspired by the approach taken by crypto entrepreneur Justin Sun, who also faces US regulatory scrutiny.

CZ Shuts Down False Claims From Bloomberg After WSJ Saga

Bloomberg followed up with its own report, echoing similar unsubstantiated claims.

One of the Trump family's crypto ventures has discussed doing business with Binance, the world's largest digital-asset exchange. Its founder pleaded guilty in 2023 to failing to maintain anti-money laundering measures. https://t.co/2rJOAXM3oC

— Bloomberg Crypto (@crypto) March 13, 2025

CZ swiftly responded, calling out the publication’s history of misleading coverage about him.

4 x2 FUD, with a history 🧵

So, Bloomberg was the “another media” in my earlier post👇. They just published their baseless hit piece after WSJ, with statements:

“It's not clear what stage the discussions have reached or whether they’ll result in any transactions or ventures”… https://t.co/xV1oFme0Vz

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) March 13, 2025

He reminded the public that in July 2024, Bloomberg was forced to issue a formal apology after he sued them for defamation.

The lawsuit stemmed from a 2022 article in Bloomberg Businessweek’s Chinese edition, which falsely accused him of running a Ponzi scheme.

Notice this apology was published on their HK company’s corporate website, not the Bloomberg website where they published the original news, on the cover of the magazine.

The English (left) says "can ... stand the cold?"

The Chinese says "Zhao's ponzi scheme" 🤷‍♂️ pic.twitter.com/8BfnoGazxl

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) March 13, 2025

Bloomberg’s apology read:

“In our 250th issue (July 6, 2022), we made the egregious mistake of publishing a cover page with a headline that was false and baseless.”

Bloomberg admitted:

“We were wrong to write ‘Changpeng Zhao’s Ponzi Scheme.’ We retract and withdraw this offensive statement without reservation and apologize unreservedly for any harm or distress caused to Mr. Zhao and Binance, the organization he founded.”

However, CZ highlighted what he saw as Bloomberg’s hypocrisy in handling the apology.

Instead of giving it the same prominence as the original false report, the publication quietly posted it on a corporate website.

Now if we look at their apology text, their English version contain no mention of their own name "Bloomberg".

"In our 250th issue (6 July 2022), we made the egregious mistake of publishing a cover page with a headline that was false and baseless. We were wrong to write "…

— CZ 🔶 BNB (@cz_binance) March 13, 2025

He also noted that Bloomberg altered the original headline only after legal action was taken and that the apology itself conspicuously avoided mentioning Bloomberg by name:

“They published their false claim on the cover of their magazine, but their apology was hidden on their HK corporate website."

He argued:

“The English version of the magazine cover asked, ‘Can … stand the cold?’ But the Chinese version said, ‘Zhao’s Ponzi Scheme.’ After we filed the lawsuit, they changed it to ‘Mysterious CZ’.”

Despite this history, CZ accused the outlet of continuing to publish what he considers baseless hit pieces against him:

“Their English apology doesn’t even say ‘Bloomberg’ once. But if you Google Translate their Chinese version, it clearly says ‘Bloomberg Businessweek/Chinese Edition Apology Notice’ at the top.”

They will eat anything they said againt you, just Keep doing your job and don't give them a single minute to distract you. The rotten fruits will one day fall by them selves.

— Ashir ibrahim_10 (@Ashiribrahim01) March 13, 2025

The Impact of a Presidential Pardon on CZ’s Case

Having already served his sentence, a pardon for CZ would not erase his felony conviction, but it could lift legal restrictions tied to it.

According to the US Justice Department, a pardon can “facilitate removal of legal disabilities imposed because of the conviction,” potentially allowing CZ to re-engage with the crypto industry.

With two major news outlets reporting similar claims, the question remains—Is CZ simply pushing back against misinformation, or is he preemptively denying details that surfaced ahead of schedule?

data.title: Logical analysis of Binance’s reduction of holdings and MGX investment: valuation, motivation and market impact
data.description:
Author: Biupa-TZC, Twitter丨@biupa

As the market gradually cools down, Binance announced today that MGX has invested in Binance. Combined with the objective fact that Binance sold a large number of crypto assets in January, I analyzed the possible investment logic of MGX, the motivation of Binance's reduction of holdings, and the market impact, hoping to provide a logical explanation for a series of events.

1.Valuation of Minority Equity Investment

During my time working at a foreign investment bank, I participated in a minority equity investment transaction (Minority Stakernvestment Deal). In the transaction at that time, the buyer was a European pension fund asset management institution, the seller was a Chinese enterprise, and our bank served as the buyer's advisor (Buyside Advisory).

In this type of investment transaction, the buyer needs to determine the valuation of the target company, so a complex valuation model is usually constructed.

At that time, we used an Excel model containing dozens of worksheets (Tab) to perform valuation calculations to ensure that the transaction price was reasonable.

Since the buyer wants to buy at a low price and the seller wants to sell at a high price, the two parties usually need to conduct multiple rounds of negotiations to reach a consensus on the transaction price.

From the perspective of investment logic, the process of MGX investing in a minority stake in Binance is likely to be similar. In the transaction, MGX needs to model the valuation of Binance and negotiate with Binance on valuation and investment conditions.

The transaction price/valuation will be the core issue of the entire transaction. If the buyer can lower the price, it can increase the return on investment (IRR/MOIC); if the seller can raise the price, it can increase its own income. The two have a conflict of interest, which is why I think Binance sold off in January.

II.MGX's background and investment methods

MGX is an investment platform jointly established by Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala and technology group G42, with the attributes of a semi-sovereign fund. Mubadala is known for its high salaries, and most of its staff members come from foreign investment banks in Europe/Hong Kong/the United States, and some have jumped from private equity funds (Mega Fund), so the team is extremely professional.

According to the normal process of private equity investment (PE), MGX will also conduct a detailed valuation before investing in Binance.

The final transaction price has not been disclosed to the public, and only professional institutions such as investment banks, lawyers, and auditors related to the transaction can see the specific data. Common calculation methods may include P/E, EV/EBITDA, EV/AUM, etc. Here you can refer to how American sell-side analysts value Coinbase, the logic is similar.

3. Binance's asset composition and reduction amount

In the valuation of traditional enterprises, assets mainly refer to fixed assets, and will be evaluated by professional institutions (such as JLL, Cushman & Wakefield). As the world's largest crypto trading platform, Binance's asset side includes not only fixed assets, but also a large number of cryptocurrency holdings.

According to Binance's public proof of reserves, its own assets can be calculated by subtracting "customer net balance" from "Er'an Wallet Balance". In 2025

  • January 1: Binance holds about 50,000 BTC, 3 billion USDT, 220,000 ETH, 6 million BNB, 450,000 SOL, 70 million FDUSD, 100 million XRP, 700 million USDC, etc.

  • February 1: Binance holds about 2,700 BTC, 270 million USDT, 150 ETH, 5 million BNB, 4,000 SOL, 30 million FDUSD, 90 million XRP, 1.3 billion USDC, etc.

According to the data, Binance significantly reduced its holdings in January by

  • 50,000 BTC (about 5 billion US dollars)

  • 220,000 ETH (about 700 million US dollars)

  • 1 million BNB (about 600 million US dollars

  • 450,000 SOL (about 100 million US dollars)

  • USDT decreased by 2.7 billion, USDC increased by 1.3 billion, and the total stablecoin net reduction was 1.4 billion US dollars

A total of about 8 billion US dollars in crypto assets were reduced.

Fourth, possible reasons for Binance's large-scale reduction

Binance reduced its holdings of about $8 billion in crypto assets in January, accounting for the majority of its holdings. I think there are three main possible reasons

1. Facilitate valuation confirmation and reduce the impact of price fluctuations

Cryptocurrency prices fluctuate greatly, with a daily fluctuation of 5%-10%, and liquidity discounts are obvious. Cleaning up crypto assets before trading can help reduce valuation uncertainty and make it easier for both parties to reach a consensus.

2. Match MGX's investment preferences

3. Dividends to old shareholders

Binance may use part of the cashed-out funds to pay dividends to old shareholders by reducing its holdings of crypto assets, so that old shareholders can receive cash before the transaction. MGX can acquire shares in Binance's core business at a lower price, achieving a win-win situation.

V. Market Impact Analysis

Binance's reduction of holdings had a certain impact on the crypto market in January

  • BTC:The total net inflow of ETFs in January was US$5.25 billion, and Binance's reduction of BTC was worth US$5 billion, which basically offset each other

  • ETH:The total net outflow of ETFs in January was US$660 million, and Binance reduced its holdings by US$700 million, which was basically equal to doubling the ETF outflow

If Binance had not carried out this round of reduction, the closing prices of BTC and ETH in January might have been higher than 102,500 and 3,300. BTC may also hit an ATH price higher than 109,000 in January.

However, since most altcoins (including ETH) have fallen in mid-to-late December, it is still unknown whether the market can reach a new high in January even without the selling pressure from Binance.

My estimate is that without the selling pressure from Binance, ETH tends to close in the red in January (above 3,300), but the closing price may be lower than the opening price of 3,700 in December. Shansai may also rise slightly overall from December 31, but still lower than the high point on December 7.

The decline in February was mainly due to the tariff/recession uncertainty brought about by the Trump administration, which has little to do with the selling pressure on Binance

Six, Future Outlook

After the investment in MGX, I think it is neutral or positive for the currency market as a whole

  • MGX uses stablecoins to invest in Binance, which means that Binance has added stablecoins on its asset side. These stablecoins may be used for market-making lending/repurchasing cryptocurrencies, thereby improving the liquidity of the currency circle

  • If MGX requires Binance to no longer hold cryptocurrencies and only operate as a pure exchange, because there is almost no cryptocurrency in Binance's existing assets, this will no longer have a negative impact on the future market

  • If Binance sold the coins just for the convenience of valuation and the need for shareholder dividends, it still has plans to enter the cryptocurrency market in the future. As the market gradually fell/bottomed out in March, it is possible to buy them back as a potential bargain-hunting force, which is potentially beneficial for the bottoming out and rebound in the future.

As a private enterprise, Binance really has no obligation to tell us in advance about the sale of coins in January. This is not the only example of information asymmetry that retail investors suffer in this market.
